mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, directly boosting S&P 500 forward returns over a one-month horizon.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.02 over a 3-year rolling window or positive forward returns occur in less than 55% of rate-cut months.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly unemployment rate increase exceeding 0.3 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions that compress consumer spending expectations and trigger negative S&P 500 returns within 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in over 60% of triggered months or the signal loses statistical significance (p > 0.10) in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates synchronized consumer and business confidence, generating sustained positive equity returns over a quarter as revenue growth materializes.
failure_condition: Joint positive signals yield negative 60-day returns in more than 50% of cases or interaction term coefficient is statistically insignificant.

mechanism_narrative: Sharp contractions in industrial production during late-cycle expansions trigger immediate profit margin concerns and liquidity tightening, causing negative S&P 500 returns within one week.
failure_condition: Signal fails to predict negative returns during late-cycle phases or produces positive returns in over 55% of contraction months.

mechanism_narrative: Rising inflation combined with stagnant federal funds rates erodes real yields and forces markets to price in aggressive future tightening, producing negative forward equity returns over 20 days.
failure_condition: Equity returns remain positive in over 60% of triggered instances or the interaction effect vanishes in multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: Steady growth in total nonfarm payrolls expands aggregate income and corporate revenue baselines, driving positive S&P 500 returns over a three-month horizon as earnings reports confirm the trend.
failure_condition: Payroll growth fails to correlate with positive 60-day returns or Rank IC drops below 0.01 across rolling 2-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A quarterly real GDP contraction exceeding 0.5% triggers immediate recession fears and margin compression, causing sharp negative S&P 500 returns within five trading days.
failure_condition: Market returns remain positive in over 50% of contraction quarters or the signal shows no predictive power in walk-forward validation.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production rebounds following a monetary easing cycle signal improved capacity utilization and corporate output, generating positive equity returns over 20 days as supply chains normalize.
failure_condition: Production rebounds yield negative 20-day returns in more than 55% of cases during recovery phases or signal loses significance.
